_comment: Source: Michigan MDHHS Section 8-15(S) Enhanced Paramedic / Critical Care Paramedic Interfacility (Revised 4/28/2023, MDHHS Reviewed 2023). Base Michigan template — MCA-specific selections not configured.
level_display: System (Enhanced Paramedic / Critical Care Paramedic — MCA Optional)
condition: Enhanced Paramedic / Critical Care Paramedic Interfacility Transport
protocol_reference: Michigan System Protocol Section 8-15(S): Enhanced Paramedic/Critical Care Paramedic Interfacility — MDHHS Approved 4/28/2023
purpose: MCA OPTIONAL. Govern the expanded scope of Enhanced Paramedic (EP) and Critical Care Paramedic (CCP) providers during interfacility transport. EPs and CCPs may carry and administer medications and perform procedures beyond the base Paramedic scope when authorized by MCA. MCAs must define the specific expanded scope items authorized for EP/CCP interfacility transport in their jurisdiction.

sections.title: Authorization
points: MCA OPTIONAL — only authorized if MCA has approved EP/CCP expanded scope for interfacility transport
Providers must hold current Enhanced Paramedic (EP) or Critical Care Paramedic (CCP) licensure/credentialing
MCAs must define which specific expanded scope items are authorized — EP/CCP providers may only perform MCA-authorized items
MCA authorization does NOT extend to skills not specifically listed in MCA protocols

sections.title: Potential Expanded Scope Items (MCA Selects)
points: Blood product administration (PRBCs, FFP, platelets): requires MCA authorization and specific EP/CCP training
Vasoactive medication initiation (vasopressors, inotropes): requires MCA authorization
Arterial line monitoring and management: requires MCA authorization
Advanced sedation and pain management medications beyond base Paramedic scope: requires MCA authorization
Invasive hemodynamic monitoring: requires MCA authorization
Advanced airway management beyond base scope: requires MCA authorization
Specific MCA-defined critical care medications: requires MCA authorization and documented training

sections.title: Standards of Practice
points: EP/CCP providers must practice within their credentialed scope and MCA authorization
EP/CCP providers must have completed documented training for each authorized expanded scope item
MCAs must maintain records of EP/CCP credentialing and authorized scope items
EP/CCP expanded scope is only authorized during interfacility transport — NOT for 9-1-1 primary responses unless specifically authorized by MCA
When operating beyond base Paramedic scope, EP/CCP providers must document expanded scope interventions and rationale in PCR

mca_selections_required.description: MCA must authorize Enhanced Paramedic or Critical Care Paramedic expanded scope for interfacility transport
options: Authorized — EP/CCP expanded scope for interfacility transport

mca_selections_required.description: MCA may authorize EP/CCP to administer blood products during interfacility transport
options: Authorized — EP/CCP may administer blood products with documented training

mca_selections_required.description: MCA may authorize EP/CCP to initiate vasoactive medications during interfacility transport
options: Authorized — EP/CCP may initiate vasoactive medications per MCA formulary

mca_selections_required.description: MCA may authorize EP/CCP to manage arterial lines during interfacility transport
options: Authorized — EP/CCP may manage and monitor arterial lines

deterioration_flags: EP/CCP requested to perform skill outside MCA authorization — decline; perform within authorized scope; contact Medical Control
Blood product reaction (fever, chills, back pain, urticaria, hypotension) — stop transfusion immediately; maintain IV access; contact Medical Control; manage per reaction type
scope_notes: MCA OPTIONAL — EP or CCP licensure/credentialing required
MCA must define specific authorized expanded scope items
Expanded scope: blood products, vasoactives, arterial lines, advanced sedation, hemodynamic monitoring
MCA authorization required for each expanded scope item
Documented training required for each authorized expanded scope item
Primarily for interfacility transport — 9-1-1 use requires separate MCA authorization
Document all expanded scope interventions in PCR
